channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Navigating the Challenges of Italian Trains: A Traveler's Experience
item.description: The article recounts a traveler's frustrating experience with Italian trains, highlighting issues such as confusing announcements, frequent platform changes, and logistical chaos. The author describes...

item.title: Senate impeachment court says Sara Duterte cannot be required to personally attend impeachment proceedings
item.description: The spokesperson of the Senate impeachment court said that Vice President Sara Duterte cannot be compelled to personally attend her impeachment trial because she is protected by her constitutional rig...

item.title: Tanzania Minister Urges Active Parenting to Shape Nation's Future
item.description: Dr Dorothy Gwajima, Tanzania's Minister for Community Development, Gender, Women and Special Groups, emphasized that the future of the nation hinges on how children are raised. Speaking at the Nationa...

item.title: Former Indiana Governor Supports AI Training Programs for U.S. Workers
item.description: Former Indiana Governor Eric Holcomb has publicly endorsed initiatives aimed at preparing U.S. workers for the evolving landscape of artificial intelligence (AI). The article highlights his support fo...

item.title: Canada Selects German Firm to Build Up to 12 Submarines in Major Defence Procurement
item.description: The Canadian government has finalized a deal with Germany’s ThyssenKrupp Marine Systems (TKMS) to build up to 12 submarines, marking the largest defence procurement in Canadian history. Prime Minister...

item.title: NASENI and CCB Partner to Digitize Asset Declarations, Boost Transparency in Public Service
item.description: The National Agency for Science and Engineering Infrastructure (NASENI) and the Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB) have partnered to modernize asset declaration processes for public officers, aiming to enha...

item.title: Karnataka CM Shivakumar Denies Govt Interference in SIR, Accuses Opposition of Political Tactics
item.description: Karnataka Chief Minister D K Shivakumar denied allegations of government interference in the ongoing electoral roll verification (SIR) process, accusing the BJP and JD(S) of using the issue for politi...

item.title: Teen Denied Legal Abortion Due to Religious Objections Seeks Unsafe Procedure in Limpopo
item.description: An 18-year-old from rural Limpopo, South Africa, was denied a legal abortion at a public clinic after healthcare workers refused care on religious grounds, forcing her to seek an illegal procedure fro...

item.title: FIFA Lifts Balogun's Suspension Amid Trump's Influence and World Cup Controversy
item.description: The decision by FIFA to lift the suspension of U.S. player Folarin Balogun has sparked significant controversy, particularly with Belgium, the U.S.'s next World Cup opponent. Balogun, who received a r...

item.title: Australian filmmaker uses AI to create groundbreaking wildlife documentary
item.description: Jodie Heenan's AI-generated documentary 'Guardians of the Burrow' won an award at the Omni AI film festival, showcasing how artificial intelligence can capture intricate natural interactions. The film...

item.title: UNICEF reports over 300 children killed or injured in Sudan conflict within six months
item.description: A United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) report has revealed that more than 300 children have been killed or injured in the ongoing conflict in Sudan within a six-month period. The findings, released...

item.title: Nuclear Waste Watch: The Oneness of Humanity
item.description: The article discusses the importance of addressing nuclear waste management and promoting a radiation-free environment through global cooperation. It highlights initiatives like the Golden Rule Projec...

item.title: NCRPO Maintains Full Alert During VP Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial
item.description: The National Capital Region Police Office (NCRPO) has declared a state of full alert in Metro Manila as the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte commenced on July 6, 2026. Over 4,000 polic...

item.title: New Rural Employment Scheme Focuses on Water Security and Disaster Mitigation
item.description: The new rural employment scheme VB-G RAM G, launched from July 1, emphasizes strengthening water security in villages by allocating 107 of 318 permissible works to address rain deficiency issues. This...

item.title: Jordan Henderson's Wrist Injury Raises Doubts Over World Cup Participation
item.description: England midfielder Jordan Henderson is likely to undergo surgery for a wrist injury sustained during a celebratory fall after his team's World Cup victory over Mexico. The 36-year-old player fell awkw...

item.title: Trump's Hypocrisy on Birthright Citizenship Exposed Through Balogun's Case
item.description: The article discusses the irony of US President Donald Trump's stance on birthright citizenship through the case of Folarin Balogun, a US World Cup striker. Balogun, who became an American citizen due...

item.title: Western Cape Concludes Repatriation Efforts Amid Ongoing Stranding of Foreign Nationals
item.description: The Western Cape government has officially concluded its voluntary repatriation operation for foreign nationals, yet media reports indicate that hundreds remain stranded outside the Department of Home...

item.title: NGE Hosts 4th Lateef Jakande Memorial Lecture to Discuss Media and Election Integrity
item.description: The Nigerian Guild of Editors (NGE) is set to host the 4th Lateef Jakande Memorial Lecture on July 23, 2026, at the Sheraton Hotel in Ikeja, Lagos. The event, themed 'The Media, INEC, Voters and Path ...

item.title: Spur workers allege exploitative conditions and denied rights at Cape Town franchise
item.description: Workers at a Spur franchise in Cape Town have accused their employer of systemic labor violations, including poverty wages, excessive working hours, and denial of basic employment rights. Current and ...

item.title: Delhi High Court asks WFI to resolve show-cause notice against Vinesh Phogat within two weeks
item.description: The Delhi High Court has directed the Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) to resolve the show-cause notice issued to wrestler Vinesh Phogat within two weeks. The notice was issued over allegations of ...
